text: The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. Make sure to bring an appropriate adapter.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius. Make sure to pack accordingly.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
Remember, the “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” in Danish is “farvel”, and “goodbye” in Danish is “tak”.
Amarienborg
It is the home of the Danish royal family. It's a must-visit for anyone interested in royal history. Plus, there's a change of guard at 12:00 which is a sight to behold.
$1.25 (Reservation required)
All week
Frederiks Kirke
This is a famous modern architecture. Its vast dome is one of the largest in northern Europe.
Tuesday to Friday
The Little Mermaid Statue
This is a famous statue based on the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales. It's one of the most iconic symbols of the city.
Free
Noma
It's a world-famous restaurant that offers culinary masterpieces. A real treat for foodies.
Rundetaarn
This is an Astronomy tower from 17th century, offering a panoramic view of the city.
Christiansborg Palace
This is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
Tuesday to Sunday
